verdict.label: YELLOW · Half size, A-grade only
verdict.desc: The tape flipped calmer overnight: June CPI came in soft (−0.4% m/m, 3.5% y/y vs 3.8% expected), July Fed-hike odds collapsed from 42% to 17%, and Trump dropped the 20% Hormuz cargo toll — so oil eased and the VIX fell back to ~16.5. But June PPI drops at 8:30 AM ET today (within the hour), and a major inflation print inside 24h locks out a GREEN verdict under the master rules. Composite cooled to 44 (MILD) and P(≥1% gap) is 32% — just under the 35% trigger. The disciplined read: half size, A-grade only, 7-DTE that clears PPI, and wait until the 8:30 number settles before deploying.

focus.jbig: PPI at 8:30 · CPI cooled · oil eased · half size only
focus.jtag: Soft CPI + Hormuz toll dropped calmed the tape — but June PPI lands at 8:30, so stay disciplined
focus.jnote: The environment cooled overnight — for the better, but not all-clear. June CPI landed soft Tuesday (headline −0.4% m/m, 3.5% y/y vs 3.8% expected), which sent July Fed-hike odds tumbling from 42% to about 17% and lifted risk appetite. President Trump also abandoned the 20% Strait of Hormuz cargo toll, replacing it with trade-and-investment deals — oil eased off its highs (WTI ~$79.56, Brent ~$85). The VIX fell back to ~16.5. But June PPI prints at 8:30 AM ET today, with the Beige Book and Fed speakers (Williams, Cook) plus Warsh’s testimony to follow. This is a scheduled-data morning stacked on a still-live (if de-escalating) Iran backdrop — deploy carefully, not aggressively.
focus.notdown: PayPal (PYPL) is the day’s cleanest news gap — up ~18–20% premarket on a $53B / $60.50-a-share Stripe–Advent takeover bid. That’s a durable, specific M&A re-rate (Type B) with a hard deal floor beneath it. Tech/AI names and financials are also catching a post-CPI bid. These are the most tradable longs, but confirm the gap holds through 9:35 and keep size at half into PPI.
focus.notup: Rate-sensitive laggards and inflation-hedge trades (some energy, gold miners) are softer as the inflation scare fades and the dollar firms. But selling bear calls the same morning as a PPI print carries squeeze risk on any soft number — the disciplined answer is to wait for 8:30 to clear before opening anything bearish.
focus.why: Why YELLOW? Composite cooled to 44 (MILD) and P(≥1% gap) at 32% is just under the 35% trigger — both would allow GREEN on their own. What locks GREEN out is the rule: a major inflation print (PPI) inside the next 24h — in fact within the hour — forces at least YELLOW. Add a still-live, though de-escalating, Iran/Hormuz situation. The play: half size, A-grade only, 7-DTE that clears the data, and ideally wait for the 8:30 number and the 9:35 validation check before deploying.

how_built.name: Volatility — VIX ~16.5, down ~4% off the 17.16 CPI-day close as the inflation scare fades
Big events (next 1–3 days) — June PPI + Beige Book + Fed speakers today; UAL/MS/JNJ/BLK earnings; NFLX later this week
World risk — Iran/Hormuz de-escalating; Trump dropped the 20% cargo toll, but the conflict is still live
Volatility trend — front-month VIX falling back toward 3-month; curve normalizing off stress
Commodities — oil eased off highs as the Hormuz toll was dropped; still ~$80 WTI, a swing factor

snapshot.delta: eased · July hike odds fell to 17%

setups.read: The day’s cleanest Sniper: a $53B / $60.50-a-share Stripe–Advent takeover bid gapped PYPL ~18–20% premarket off a ~$47 close. This is a specific, durable M&A re-rate (Type B) with a hard deal-price floor beneath the stock — the ideal “line in the sand” for a bull put. Two cautions: the offer is unsolicited (PayPal hasn’t responded), so a rejection or deal-break gaps it back, and a PPI print lands the same morning. Grade B, not A, on that headline dependence.
setups.check_note: Pre-9:35. Confirm PYPL holds above prior close & open, upper third of range at 9:35; half size only, short put well below the deal price. Wait for PPI at 8:30 first.

setups.read: Chips rebounded Tuesday (SMH ~+2.5%) and futures firmed as the soft CPI cut Fed-hike odds. The macro lean is friendlier, but this is a broad sentiment bounce, not a single-name news gap — and the group whipsawed hard all week on the AI-spend scare. No clean per-stock catalyst plus a PPI print pending = stand aside on new premium here today.
setups.check_note: A bounce isn’t a catalyst. Don’t sell tech premium into an 8:30 PPI print; revisit once the number clears.

setups.read: Morgan Stanley beat handily ($3.46 vs ~$2.94 est) and traded up ~1% premarket; BLK also reports today. A beat-and-hold could set up a Type A, but the reactions are muted and PPI lands the same morning — not a clean, table-pounding gap yet. Let the reaction and the 8:30 print settle before considering anything.
setups.check_note: Muted beat reaction + PPI in the window = wait. Re-check MS after the number and the 9:35 validation.

setups.read: United reports after the close today, so earnings risk is ahead, not behind — that disqualifies it as a Type A right now and blocks any spread whose DTE spans the print. Jet fuel eased with oil off its Hormuz peak, but that’s not a fresh tradable gap. Check back once UAL has reported and gapped.
setups.check_note: Earnings tonight = do not open any UAL spread. Nothing to validate pre-print.

setups.read: The refiners (VLO, MPC, PSX, PARR) printed all-time highs Tuesday on wide crack spreads — a real breakout theme — but it’s an oil-linked move that eased once the Hormuz toll was dropped, so the pivot may not hold into a de-escalating tape and a PPI print. No clean, durable breakout worth underwriting a bull put into this morning. Watch-only.
setups.check_note: Refiner highs are crack-spread/oil driven — too headline-dependent to take. Nothing holding a clean pivot to check.

scenarios.label: Most likely — Soft PPI, risk-on drift
scenarios.sub: PPI lands near or below consensus, the CPI relief extends, stocks grind higher, vol keeps easing

scenarios.label: Good case — Clean cool print + deal flow
scenarios.sub: PPI clearly cool plus M&A optimism (PYPL); a broad relief rally, VIX toward the mid-15s

scenarios.label: Bad case — Hot PPI reignites rate fear
scenarios.sub: Core PPI runs hot (est +0.4% m/m, 5.2% y/y core); yields pop, tech sells off, hike odds climb back

scenarios.label: Tail — Iran re-escalation / oil spike
scenarios.sub: The de-escalation reverses, Hormuz risk returns; oil spikes, a sharp risk-off VIX pop

risks: June PPI · today 8:30 AM ET — headline seen ~−0.1% m/m but core ~+0.4% m/m / 5.2% y/y; a hot core would undercut the CPI relief and revive rate fear. The day’s biggest scheduled catalyst — wait for it to clear before deploying.
Fed speakers & Beige Book — Williams and Cook speak, Warsh testifies, and the Beige Book lands this afternoon. Any hawkish tone stacks on the PPI number — headline risk through the session.
Iran / Strait of Hormuz — Trump dropped the 20% cargo toll and tensions eased, but the conflict is still live. Any re-escalation sends crude and jet fuel vertical again — the week’s biggest tail.
PYPL deal risk — the Stripe–Advent bid is unsolicited and PayPal hasn’t responded. A rejection or deal-break headline gaps the stock straight back down — size any spread for that.

UAL (tonight) & NFLX this week — United reports after the close and Netflix is due later in the week; single-stock earnings gaps stacked on the macro calendar. Keep spreads clear of each name’s print.

earnings.intro: Q2 season is ramping — Morgan Stanley, BlackRock and J&J reported around today, with United Airlines after the close and Netflix later this week. With PPI landing at 8:30 and the Iran backdrop still live, a single-stock earnings gap on top of macro risk is a double catalyst — keep every spread clear of its name’s print and of the data.

rows.why: Beat handily — $3.46 EPS on $21.35B revenue vs ~$2.94 / $19.64B est — and traded up ~1% premarket. A clean beat, but a muted reaction into a PPI morning is not yet a table-pounding Type A.
rows.angle: Don’t sell MS premium into the 8:30 print. A beat-and-hold could set up a Type A once the reaction and PPI settle.

rows.stock_sub: United · first airline of the season
rows.why: Reports tonight — earnings risk is ahead, not behind. Jet fuel eased as oil came off its Hormuz peak, a modest tailwind, but the guide is the swing factor. No spread whose DTE spans tonight’s print.
rows.angle: Do not carry a UAL spread through tonight. A clean gap could become a Type A tomorrow.

rows.stock_sub: Netflix · among the week’s big prints
rows.why: Netflix reports later this week; the stock carries high single-stock gap risk into a jumpy macro tape. The number, not the setup, will drive it.
rows.angle: No spread on NFLX through its print. Watch it for the read; don’t underwrite the gap.

earnings.outro: Wed 7/15: PPI at 8:30 plus Beige Book and Fed speakers headline the day; MS, BLK and J&J reported, UAL is after the close. It stays busy into next week with Netflix and more S&P names — against a de-escalating but still-live Iran backdrop. Half size, A-grade only, until the data clears.
